---
title: "Data Exploration - Worm Embryo Microscopy Images"
description: "Loading and visualizing the C. elegans test dataset"
author: "Camila Ramirez"
date: "2026-03-04"
categories: [data exploration, microscopy, python]
draft: false
---

## Introduction
In this post I load and explore the test microscopy images 
provided by the lab.

## Loading the Images
```{python}
import tifffile
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Load both images
spima = tifffile.imread('../../data/Test_Data/input/SPIMA/SPIMA-80.tif')
spimb = tifffile.imread('../../data/Test_Data/input/SPIMB/SPIMB-80.tif')

print(f"SPIMA shape: {spima.shape}")
print(f"SPIMB shape: {spimb.shape}")
print(f"Data type: {spima.dtype}")
```

## Visualizing the Images
```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5))

axes[0].imshow(spima[spima.shape[0]//2], cmap='gray')
axes[0].set_title('SPIMA - Middle Slice')
axes[0].axis('off')

axes[1].imshow(spimb[spimb.shape[0]//2], cmap='gray')
axes[1].set_title('SPIMB - Middle Slice')
axes[1].axis('off')

plt.suptitle('C. elegans Embryo - Raw Microscopy Images')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
